prompt: A classic 1960s Op Art (Optical Art) masterpiece in the style of Bridget Riley or Victor Vasarely. A precise abstract geometric composition using exclusively stark black and crisp white paint. At the outer perimeter, bold, high-contrast concentric zigzag patterns are intensely clear. As the patterns plunge into the deep center, the alternating stripes exponentially shrink, compress, and crowd together until they completely dissolve into an indistinguishable, muddy grey blur at the core, permanently losing all high-contrast signal.
tradition_approach: Employed Op Art's precise, high-contrast black-and-white hard-edged geometry, where bold exterior patterns recursively compress into indistinguishable grey static at the deep center—perfectly mimicking how gradients exponentially decay and lose all signal in deep architectures.
